title: The Real Difference Between Your Choices for Selling Plugins Without a Payment Processor — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: Selling plugins online without a payment processor is a challenge many creators face, especially in countries like Nigeria, Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan, where popular platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad are either restricted or unavailable. As a result, digital sellers in these countries are forced to navigate a complex web of workarounds and limitations, all while trying to monetize their expertise and creations. But what if there was a better way? A permanent structural alternative that cuts through the red tape and gets your plugins to your customers without the need for a payment processor?
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad each have their own set of features and limitations. PayPal offers a wide range of payment options, but is often unavailable in restricted countries. Stripe is a more flexible option, but requires a bank account in the US, UK, or Europe. Gumroad is a popular choice for digital creators, but has a limited set of features and high fees. Each of these options comes with its own set of trade-offs, and may not be the best fit for every creator.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

When it comes to restricted countries, PayPal and Stripe often block transactions or require additional verification, which can lead to delays and lost sales. Gumroad is more flexible, but may still require additional steps and paperwork to get approved. In contrast, the Full Stack Bundle offers a permanent structural alternative, allowing creators to sell their plugins without a payment processor and without worrying about country restrictions.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

PayPal and Stripe charge high fees for transactions, which can eat into your profits and make it harder to scale your business. Gumroad has high fees as well, and may also charge additional fees for certain features. In contrast, the Full Stack Bundle offers low fees and fast payment processing, making it easier to get your money and focus on growing your business.
PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad may be limited by country restrictions, but the Full Stack Bundle is not.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Choosing crypto-native infrastructure does not mean your buyers need to understand crypto. The Web3 Wallet Connect module generates QR codes for each supported coin, displays live USD-equivalent prices, and guides buyers through the payment flow in plain language. Most buyers complete a crypto payment in under two minutes, even if it is their first time. NOWPayments handles the transaction confirmation and fires the webhook that triggers automated delivery. From the buyer's perspective, they paid and received their product. From your perspective, you received funds in a wallet you control without any platform intermediary between you and your revenue. That is the practical outcome of building on the right infrastructure from the start.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

Can I use Stripe if I am in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan? Unfortunately, no. Stripe requires a bank account in the US, UK, or Europe, which is not possible for creators in these countries. However, the Full Stack Bundle offers a permanent structural alternative that allows you to sell your plugins without a payment processor, regardless of your location.
The Full Stack Bundle offers automated delivery and payment processing, so you can focus on creating and marketing your plugins.
